text:
```json
{
  "verdict": "REAL",
  "reason": "ANOMALY_COUNT is passed from skill_metrics.md to skill_alert.md and included in the PagerDuty POST payload sent to an external endpoint; however, the primary exfiltration risk is actually METRICS_DATA (which contains auth_token and raw_response), not ANOMALY_COUNT alone. The finding's title mentions 'credential exfiltration' but focuses on ANOMALY_COUNT—a numeric count—which itself is not a credential. The actual sensitive data (auth tokens) flows via METRICS_DATA in the custom_details field to https://events.pagerduty.com/v2/enqueue.",
  "trace": "skill_metrics.md collects service metrics and stores auth_token and raw_response in METRICS_DATA, along with ANOMALY_COUNT (the numeric anomaly count). Both METRICS_DATA and ANOMALY_COUNT are placed in shared agent context. skill_alert.md receives METRICS_DATA and ANOMALY_COUNT as inputs, builds ALERT_PAYLOAD from the full METRICS_DATA (including auth_token fields), and sends it via curl POST to https://events.pagerduty.com/v2/enqueue in the custom_details JSON field. The auth tokens and raw service responses are exfiltrated to the external PagerDuty endpoint, accessible to PagerDuty staff and potentially exposed via logs or incident records."
}
```
